---
title: "About"
---

# About Me

I am **Md Mejanur Rahman Mejan**, an undergraduate student pursuing dual bachelor's degrees in **Political Science** and **International Studies** at the **University of Idaho**, with an expected graduation date of **May 2027**.

My research focuses on **political behavior, public opinion, electoral legitimacy, democratic institutions, political psychology, comparative politics, public policy, and human rights**. I am particularly interested in understanding how citizens evaluate democratic institutions, perceive political threats, and form attitudes toward elections and governance.

My work combines **quantitative and qualitative research methods**, including survey analysis, statistical modeling, and experimental research design. I have presented research at regional academic conferences and continue to develop projects using large public opinion datasets and modern computational tools.

Outside of research, I enjoy learning new analytical methods, programming, and collaborating on interdisciplinary projects that connect political science, public policy, and data science.

---

# Research Interests

- Political Behavior
- Public Opinion
- Electoral Legitimacy
- Democratic Institutions
- Political Psychology
- Comparative Politics
- Public Policy
- Human Rights

---

# Education

## University of Idaho

**Bachelor of Science**

- Political Science
- International Studies

**Expected Graduation:** May 2027

---

# Research & Technical Skills

### Research

- Quantitative Research
- Qualitative Research
- Survey Research
- Experimental Research Design
- Literature Review
- Statistical Analysis
- Regression Modeling
- Data Visualization
- Academic Writing

### Programming & Software

- R
- Python
- SPSS
- Stata
- SQL
- Microsoft Excel
- LaTeX
- Overleaf
- Git & GitHub

---

# Conference Presentations

- Idaho Political Science Association Annual Conference
- Pacific Northwest Political Science Association (PNWPSA)
- National Model United Nations (NMUN)

---

# Languages

| Language | Proficiency |
|-----------|-------------|
| Bengali | Native |
| English | Fluent |
| Hindi | Professional |
| Urdu | Professional |
| Arabic | Basic |

---

# Current Research

My current research explores topics including:

- Electoral legitimacy and democratic attitudes
- Political behavior and public opinion
- Political psychology
- Democratic institutions
- Public policy
- Comparative politics
- Human rights

I am always interested in collaborating on research related to political science, public policy, and quantitative methods.
